items.summary: Parliamentary library data reveals the federal government has almost 300 active contracts with accounting firm KPMG, which is being investigated over claims it misused confidential client information.

items.title: Inquiry examining NDIS spending cuts told 240,000 people would exit the scheme
items.summary: More than 240,000 people would exit the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS) over four years according to modelling as part of a plan to cut spending on the multi-billion-dollar program.

items.title: Liberty Bell Bay workers given until Monday to find out if smelter will secure alternative funding
items.summary: Workers at Tasmania's troubled Liberty Bell Bay smelter have been told administrators have until Monday to secure alternative funding, after a member of the preferred consortium bidding to take over the manganese smelter effectively pulled out.

items.title: World Cup quick hits: Opening ceremony delights, hydration break drama and Haiti's jersey ban
items.summary: The 2026 FIFA World Cup is up and running, launched by a colourful opening ceremony and comprehensive win for Mexico. Here are the quick hits from the opening day of the tournament.

items.title: VCE students, parents 'shocked' as Chinese language school enters administration
items.summary: Dozens of students completing VCE Chinese language subjects face an uncertain future after a Chinese community language school goes into administration.

items.title: NT government questioned over youth justice facility lockdowns, police policy and housing in first week of budget estimates
items.summary: As the NT's CLP government remains firm on its tough-on-crime agenda and rebuilding of the economy, the first week of budget estimates saw the government questioned around prison lockdowns, police policy and housing.

items.title: NT government says land-clearing dispute with Claravale Station has reached settlement, a year after first announcing agreement
items.summary: For years, the Northern Territory government has been locked in a legal battle with Claravale Station over allegations its owners cleared hundreds of hectares without a permit. But it is not the first time the government has announced the settlement deal.

items.title: Gus Lamont's grandparent fined over gun offence unrelated to 4yo boy's disappearance
items.summary: The grandparent of missing four-year-old Gus Lamont has been fined $10,500 for possession of a gun silencer at the family's Oak Park Station, in South Australia's far north. Police have previously said the offence is not related to his disappearance.

items.title: What are the rules around using fire pits at home?
items.summary: There are rules when it comes to using fire pits and things to be cautious about, including open-air fires being a nuisance to your neighbours.

items.title: Tasmanian Premier Jeremy Rockliff answers questions over Madeleine Ogilvie's legal fees saga
items.summary: Tasmanian Premier Jeremy Rockliff has provided answers to a series of questions about former minister Madeleine Ogilvie's legal fees matter, but the Greens say they "paint a far worse picture of this shameful saga and his role in it".

items.title: Pig Feast explores Indigenous land rights in West Papua but key leader Mama Yasinta changed her tune
items.summary: A key Indigenous leader in West Papua appeared in a documentary criticising an Indonesian government program, but then changed sides and disappeared. Her family suspects foul play.

items.title: Shakira, Andrea Bocelli and Salma Hayek kickstart World Cup at opening ceremony
items.summary: The World Cup 2026 opens with a vibrant ceremony as Shakira, Andrea Bocelli and a cavalcade of Latino stars blend with Indigenous Mexican displays at the Estadio Azteca.

items.title: Firefighters union and secretary trying to block IBAC's Operation Richmond report
items.summary: The United Firefighters Union and its secretary Peter Marshall are behind a legal attempt to stop a report by Victoria's anti-corruption watchdog from being published.

items.title: How Mexico World Cup goal scorer Raúl Jiménez came back from horrific head injury
items.summary: In 2020, Mexico World Cup goal scorer Raúl Jiménez was lying on the turf of the Arsenal Stadium receiving oxygen after fracturing his skull and injuring his brain.

items.title: What a career in forensics looks like while solving crime in the outback
items.summary: From fingerprinting in the lab to photographing cattle from a helicopter, this forensic officer covers a beat the size of Victoria.

items.title: Childcare paedophile Ashley Paul Griffith loses appeal to reduce non-parole period
items.summary: The former childcare worker sexually abused dozens of children at centres for almost two decades.

items.title: Government agencies told to police own AI use missed first transparency test
items.summary: The ABC can reveal dozens of federal agencies missed mandatory deadlines to disclose how they are currently using AI.

items.title: Johnny Warren inspiring Socceroos coach as he prepares to make history at FIFA World Cup
items.summary: Australian soccer legend Johnny Warren once asked why Australia was content with just qualifying for a World Cup. Australia coach Tony Popovic is remembering that as he aims higher than ever before at the tournament.

items.title: Creditors of Nathan River Resources criticise NT government for lack of oversight of mining company
items.summary: In the wake of Nathan River Resources entering into voluntary administration, creditors have criticised the NT government for its lack of oversight of the struggling company. ASIC documents show the mining company owes more than $360 million.

items.title: Cartier exhibition opens at Melbourne's NGV with hundreds of items never seen in Australia
items.summary: Glittering tiaras and dazzling necklaces worn by Hollywood icons such as Elizabeth Taylor and Grace Kelly are featured in an exhibition at the National Gallery of Victoria in Melbourne.

items.title: The Australian connection behind Roxane Gay and Channing Tatum's upcoming novel
items.summary: It's possible the ball may not have got rolling on Roxane Gay and Channing Tatum's upcoming novel if it wasn't for an Australian connection. Gay will appear at Vivid Sydney in a wide-ranging discussion.

items.title: Soaring thefts force WA mandarin grower to close roadside stall after 17 years
items.summary: Mandarin grower Bruce Crain says people's growing dishonesty, including the theft of 70 bags in one weekend, makes it impossible to offer a roadside service.

items.title: ASX ends week on a high as investors await SpaceX share launch — as it happened
items.summary: The ASX end the week on a high amid hopes of a Middle East peace deal, while SpaceX's IPO prepares for lift-off on the Nasdaq. See how the trading day unfolded on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: Angus Taylor brushes away questions on One Nation preference deal — as it happened

items.title: Pastoralists reunite for friendly competition at outback gymkhana and motorkhana after welcome rain
items.summary: The trip to Marree in South Australia looked a little different for competitors in this year's annual gymkhana and motorkhana, with the outback blooming after record-breaking rain.

items.title: Former officers who blew the whistle on NSW Police's toxic culture react to report findings
items.summary: Former NSW Police officers who blew the whistle on the toxic culture of the force have welcomed an independent report "validating" their experiences.

items.title: Man fined thousands for 'silly' illegal dumping of tyres at Moonta Mines national heritage site
items.summary: A young man who could have paid $115 to discard a trailer load of tyres has been fined thousands for illegally dumping them down a mine shaft in a national heritage-listed area — and ordered to clean it up.

items.title: Tasmanian Aboriginal community calls for change after damage to heritage site at Trevallyn Reserve
items.summary: Upgrades to an existing walking and cycling path have built over what an Aboriginal organisation describes as a "significant" cultural site in northern Tasmania — and it didn't require a permit.

items.title: Queensland beekeepers desperate for new varroa mite treatments as colonies collapse
items.summary: With many managed colonies under threat from the invasive varroa mite, Queensland beekeepers are trying to find ways to protect their industry.

items.title: Australian couples opt for micro-weddings and elopements amid cost-of-living crisis
items.summary: Melbourne couple Averill and Lloyd Johnson pulled off their dream wedding for just $3,000, compared to the average wedding cost of about $38,000.

items.title: Brisbane Broncos still alive in NRL premiership race despite losing streak, says coach Michael Maguire
items.summary: Brisbane coach Michael Maguire declares the Broncos are "still well and truly alive" in the wake of their 42-point belting at the hands of South Sydney on Thursday night.

items.title: It's been two weeks since Madeleine Ogilvie resigned. What could happen when parliament returns?
items.summary: It's been two weeks since Madeleine Ogilvie resigned from Tasmanian cabinet, and the saga is reaching a tipping point. What could happen when parliament returns?

items.title: Country swimmer trains in 25m local pool to swim 45km around New York
items.summary: While training in a small NSW country town, cold-water swimmer Peta Bradley is preparing to tackle one of the world's most demanding open-water challenges, a 45-kilometre circumnavigation of Manhattan Island.

items.title: Major PFAS class action could lead to multi-billion-dollar claim figure

items.title: One year after Air India crash, victims' families wait for investigation report
items.summary: One year on from the fatal Air India crash that claimed 260 lives, families of victims are still waiting for a report into the investigation.

items.title: 'Broken promise' or not, Anthony Albanese's tax decision has helped One Nation
items.summary: The prime minister's cavalier breaking of election promises has poured kerosene on the fire under a party that can no longer be called "minor".

items.title: Privacy commissioner gags complainant over American Express data security findings
items.summary: Australia's privacy commissioner has threatened a complainant with legal action to prevent the full disclosure of the findings of a long-running investigation into American Express's information security, which found widespread technology failures.

items.title: How One Nation could disrupt Australia's progressive stronghold
items.summary: The idea that One Nation could come close to the government benches was absurd six months ago, but it is a prospect Victorian Labor MPs and insiders are increasingly discussing.

items.title: ABC More or Less quiz: World Cup edition
items.summary: Which team has won the most World Cup matches? Has anyone lost more than England? Take our World Cup ranking quiz to find out.

items.title: How you and your superannuation came to be aboard SpaceX
items.summary: If your superannuation is managed by any of Australia's big superannuation funds, chances are you're about to become a highly valued financial backer to Elon Musk.

items.title: International students in Australia face more financial pressure as currencies weaken in parts of Asia
items.summary: When Jolie Cao chose to do her undergraduate university degree in Australia, she knew it would be expensive. But an additional financial pressure has made things "really hard".

items.title: ABC news quiz: Fake weather, a musical snub, a whale graveyard and James Bond going woke
items.summary: Don't let your confidence be shaken or stirred by this week's test of how much you've been paying attention.

items.summary: Artist Abdul Abdullah says the Cronulla riots were a "moment of revelation" for many Australians as he unveils a new work for Sydney Biennale.

items.title: This man bought the land stolen from his Indigenous ancestors
items.summary: Gunditjmara man Craig Molyneux buys back land that was stolen from his ancestors and infuses the property's old church with his Indigenous culture.

items.title: US forecasters say potentially record-breaking El Niño underway
items.summary: There is a high chance this year's Pacific climate phenomenon will be among the strongest on record, which could lead to hotter temperatures across the globe.

items.title: Six legendary Australian music acts inducted into ARIA Hall of Fame after 'a pretty amazing journey'
items.summary: Gurrumul, Jenny Morris, Kate Ceberano, Spiderbait, The Living End, and Vika and Linda Bull were elevated six to top-tier status at a ceremony in Sydney as part of ARIA's 40th anniversary celebrations.

items.summary: A report into the cancelled Byron Bay Bluesfest reveals creditors are unlikely to be repaid the millions of dollars they are owed.

items.title: Thursday finance with David Chau
items.summary: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

items.title: 'Trying not to cry': Rabbitohs rise after emotional Jai Arrow moment
items.summary: Jai Arrow has inspired South Sydney to a commanding win over the Brisbane Broncos, on an emotional night at Stadium Australia. Follow live.

items.title: 'Inspirational' Dawson stars as Crows send premiership statement
items.summary: Jordan Dawson plays one of the best quarters of his career as Adelaide claims a 57-point win over the Western Bulldogs at Docklands.

items.title: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson says she takes policy advice from 'friend' Gina Rinehart
items.summary: Pauline Hanson speaks of her "respect" for billionaire Gina Rinehart , becoming emotional during a speech recalling her time in detention.

items.title: BHP facing 'historic' strike as union members back industrial action in Port Hedland
items.summary: Unionised workers at BHP's operations in Port Hedland could walk off the job as soon as next week after endorsing protected industrial action.

items.title: Adelaide lawyer Andrew Graham pleads not guilty to sexual offences
items.summary: Andrew Graham enters not guilty pleas to two counts of rape and one charge of compelling a person to sexually manipulate an offender.

items.title: Tasmanian Premier Jeremy Rockliff links Madeleine Ogilvie's court action to Integrity Commission
items.summary: Premier Jeremy Rockliff links comments made by the boss of the state's Integrity Commission to a question about former minister Madeleine Ogilvie's Supreme Court action.

items.title: Emotional Hanson blames Abbott for electoral fraud 'witch hunt'
items.summary: As Anthony Albanese stood in front of a Medicare banner and Pauline Hanson put her hand out for donations, voters could be forgiven for thinking the election was two months, not two years, away.

items.title: Andrew Mungatopi sentenced to 10 years in jail for stabbing death of Tiwi Islands sistergirl
items.summary: Andrew Mungatopi has been sentenced to 10 years and two months in prison for the fatal stabbing of his niece in a Darwin backyard in 2024.

items.title: Alleged arsonist Jonathan Rinderknecht lonely, dejected when he lit Palisades fire, court hears
items.summary: Jonathan Rinderknecht's attorney countered that a hilltop blaze was triggered by fireworks, not his client, and that the massive Palisades inferno that killed 12 people was a separate fire set by others.

items.title: RecipeTin Eats founder Nagi adopts new canine companion
items.summary: Nagi Maehashi, the founder of RecipeTin Eats, introduces her new canine companion Jaffle.

items.title: Anti-immigration figures seizing on outrage at Northern Ireland stabbing to drive people towards cause
items.summary: Billionaire Elon Musk and far-right activist Tommy Robinson have seized upon the alleged stabbing of a man in Belfast by a Sudanese immigrant to push an anti-immigration agenda.

items.title: Daylesford pub did not hold valid outdoor dining permit at time of deadly crash, court hears
items.summary: A Victorian court hears that a regional pub where five people died when a driver had a hypoglycaemic episode, crashing his car through diners, did not have a valid outdoor dining permit at the time of the crash.

items.title: Former police officer who harassed woman online sentenced to six months' prison
items.summary: A former Victoria Police officer is sentenced to six months in prison after repeatedly harassing a woman on social media.

items.title: Casey McLean's arrival to State of Origin is proof the future is now
items.summary: By the first month of his 20s, Casey McLean had already achieved more than most players do in a lifetime but ahead of his first run-on Origin match the first of a new generation of stars is still just getting started.

items.title: Adelaide men jailed for $6 million fraudulent property investment scheme
items.summary: Two men who "exploited the vulnerability" of multiple victims by swindling millions of dollars in a fraudulent property investment scheme have been jailed for the "undoubtedly serious" offending.

items.title: New York Knicks complete record NBA Finals comeback against San Antonio Spurs
items.summary: The New York Knicks complete the greatest comeback in NBA Finals history, rallying from 29 points down to beat San Antonio in game four and move to the brink of a first title in 53 years.

items.title: State and territory disability ministers lash federal government's NDIS changes
items.summary: State and territory disability ministers have accused the Albanese government of pushing on with NDIS reform that does not align with previous agreements.

items.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup kick-off times in Australian time, when to watch in Australia
items.summary: Check out when your team's matches are kicking off at the 2026 FIFA World Cup so you don't miss a match.

items.title: Girlfriend of man shot by police disputes that officers told him to drop knife
items.summary: A coronial inquest into the shooting has heard from the woman who was in a locked bathroom with her armed partner for hours of police negotiations.
